title: ardisia recurvata
intro: Ardisia recurvata, also known as the recurved ardisia or mountain ardisia, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Sapotaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Asia, particularly in countries such as China, Japan, and the Philippines. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it effective in treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. In traditional herbal medicine, ardisia recurvata is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and can also be used as a topical application to treat skin conditions and wounds.
